verses.text: Ananias keeps back part of the price; 'thou hast not lied unto men, but unto God.' Hidden falsehood. To deceive the church is to lie to God.
Ananias keeps back part of the price; 'thou hast not lied unto men, but unto God.' Hidden falsehood. To deceive the church is to lie to God.
Ananias keeps back part of the price; 'thou hast not lied unto men, but unto God.' Hidden falsehood. To deceive the church is to lie to God.
Ananias keeps back part of the price; 'thou hast not lied unto men, but unto God.' Hidden falsehood. To deceive the church is to lie to God.
He falls down dead, and great fear comes on all. Sudden judgment. God guards the purity of His new church.
He falls down dead, and great fear comes on all. Sudden judgment. God guards the purity of His new church.
His wife Sapphira, lying likewise, falls down dead and is buried by him. Shared sin, shared doom. Both agreed to tempt the Spirit.
His wife Sapphira, lying likewise, falls down dead and is buried by him. Shared sin, shared doom. Both agreed to tempt the Spirit.
His wife Sapphira, lying likewise, falls down dead and is buried by him. Shared sin, shared doom. Both agreed to tempt the Spirit.
His wife Sapphira, lying likewise, falls down dead and is buried by him. Shared sin, shared doom. Both agreed to tempt the Spirit.
Great fear comes; many signs are done, and multitudes of believers are added. Awe and growth. Holy fear and great grace go together.
Great fear comes; many signs are done, and multitudes of believers are added. Awe and growth. Holy fear and great grace go together.
Great fear comes; many signs are done, and multitudes of believers are added. Awe and growth. Holy fear and great grace go together.
Great fear comes; many signs are done, and multitudes of believers are added. Awe and growth. Holy fear and great grace go together.
They bring the sick that Peter's shadow might fall on them, and all are healed. Abundant power. God's mercy overflows through His apostles.
They bring the sick that Peter's shadow might fall on them, and all are healed. Abundant power. God's mercy overflows through His apostles.
Imprisoned, they are freed by an angel, 'Go... speak... all the words of this life.' No prison holds them. God delivers His servants to keep preaching.
Imprisoned, they are freed by an angel, 'Go... speak... all the words of this life.' No prison holds them. God delivers His servants to keep preaching.

Imprisoned, they are freed by an angel, 'Go... speak... all the words of this life.' No prison holds them. God delivers His servants to keep preaching.
The officers find the prison empty but the apostles teaching; they bring them gently, fearing the people. Useless bars. The word cannot be locked away.
The officers find the prison empty but the apostles teaching; they bring them gently, fearing the people. Useless bars. The word cannot be locked away.

Did we not forbid you? ye intend to bring this man's blood on us. Guilty fear. They dread the very name they shed blood to silence.
Did we not forbid you? ye intend to bring this man's blood on us. Guilty fear. They dread the very name they shed blood to silence.
'We ought to obey God rather than men'; God exalted Jesus, 'a Prince and a Saviour, for to give repentance.' Higher allegiance. The apostles answer to God first.
'We ought to obey God rather than men'; God exalted Jesus, 'a Prince and a Saviour, for to give repentance.' Higher allegiance. The apostles answer to God first.

They would slay them, but Gamaliel cites Theudas and Judas, whose works failed. Wise caution. Past pretenders warn against rash action.
They would slay them, but Gamaliel cites Theudas and Judas, whose works failed. Wise caution. Past pretenders warn against rash action.

If this be of men, it will come to nought; 'if it be of God, ye cannot overthrow it.' Sound counsel. No work of God can be undone by men.
If this be of men, it will come to nought; 'if it be of God, ye cannot overthrow it.' Sound counsel. No work of God can be undone by men.
Beaten and freed, they rejoice 'that they were counted worthy to suffer shame for his name,' and cease not to preach. Joyful suffering. They count persecution an honour.
